title: The Pearl
author: John Steinbeck
content: “It is not good to want a thing too much. It sometimes drives the luck away.”

title: For Whom the Bell Tolls
author: Ernest Hemingway
content: “The world is a fine place and worth the fighting for and I hate very much to leave it.”
action: Define what is worth fighting for.

title: The House of Mirth
author: Edith Wharton
content: “I have tried to look into my own heart, and I have not found any clear answer.”

title: In Cold Blood
author: Truman Capote
content: “The village of Holcomb stands on the high wheat plains of western Kansas, a lonesome area that other Kansans call 'out there'.”

title: Their Eyes Were Watching God
author: Zora Neale Hurston
content: “There are years that ask questions and years that answer.”
action: Wait for the cycle to turn.
